change_summary: Since c3 (2026-04-17T07:14Z), WIN-015 detail_evidence expanded 51% (3765→5672 chars) adding an explicit back-of-envelope near-field mutual-inductance calculation (M≈4 nH coplanar, 8 nH coaxial; V≈20-350 mV across 0.1-1 A drive); detail_verdict_text shrunk 52% (1520→728 chars) by dropping the FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph. CUORE description corrected to neutrinoless double-beta decay. Cross-WIN FLAGGED fix also applied to WIN-003 and WIN-011 (verified). Kuhlke (JSE) citation dropped from detail_evidence but retained in detail_verdict_text without the recommended JSE caveat — partial fix.
our_argument_summary: WIN-015 claims Klaus Meyl's 'scalar waves' penetrate Faraday cages, violating standard EM. Our current c4 text: (1) acknowledges kernel of truth — near-field evanescent coupling is real, per Jackson §9.1; (2) shows Meyl's MHz apparatus operates deep inside the reactive near-field zone (d << λ/2π); (3) NEW in c4: computes expected coupling magnitude (M ≈ µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³) = 4 nH coplanar, 8 nH coaxial; V = M·2πf·I gives 20-350 mV at 0.1-1 A drive, matching Meyl's reported signals without new physics); (4) separates theory (Bruhn 2006 TU Darmstadt: Meyl's math mis-applies Pohl, violates superposition, uses E(r(t)) instead of E(r,t)) from experiment (near-field coupling, not scalar waves); (5) invokes operational Faraday shielding across CUORE (now correctly described as neutrinoless double-beta-decay search), LIGO, ADMX; (6) notes dome's codebase hardcodes status='confirmed' with no monitor.py domain. Verdict: Not Demonstrated.

relevant_files: Per prior cycles: inject_ai_layer.py (metadata-only), monitor.py (no Faraday domain; domain 8 references WIN-015 in an SAA-Decay context — copy-paste residue rather than functional integration)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: None. The dome asserts WIN-015 via bare metadata (title, Meyl as data_source, status='confirmed'). No formula links dome geometry (disc_radius=20,015; firmament_height=9,086; sun=5,733; moon=2,534) to scalar-wave penetration amplitude or frequency dependence.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded. status='confirmed' baked in at metadata-injection time; no API fetch, no comparison, no tolerance. This is a literature citation presented as a validated prediction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The current text maintains the 'bare metadata entry' framing introduced in c3. The concrete filename forensics (inject_ai_layer.py) have been generalized further in c4; a dome defender could still press for the specific line of code, but this is a minor rhetorical concession and the substantive claim is accurate (no functional monitor.py integration for Faraday-cage data).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain fetches live Faraday-cage data for WIN-015. status='confirmed' is baked into the metadata injection step. No URL, no API call, no tolerance check — confirmed by absence of any 'faraday', 'scalar', or 'meyl' domain in the automation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The dome does not rename near-field coupling as 'aetheric coupling' — it adopts Meyl's scalar-wave claim as-is. No standard phenomenon is being relabeled; the dome is importing a contested fringe claim wholesale.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Meyl's Faraday-penetration claims date 1990s-2000s. The dome model (ECM V51.1, 2025-2026) postdates this by 20-30 years. The dome cites the observation as a 'confirmed prediction' without ever making a prospective statement.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No aetheric-parameter formula produces a scalar-wave penetration amplitude. Our own text states 'no equation predicting scalar wave behaviour, no mechanism linking its cavity geometry to Faraday penetration.' Value adoption, not derivation.

holes_found.description: Kuhlke (2010) citation in Journal of Scientific Exploration remains in detail_verdict_text as a parenthetical '(Bruhn, 2006; Kuhlke, 2010)' without the JSE caveat recommended in c3. The c3 review's moderate hole #2 warned that JSE is a fringe/anomalistics venue of the same class we criticize Meyl for using, and recommended either dropping Kuhlke or annotating with an explicit caveat. The decider dropped Kuhlke from detail_evidence (full fix there) but retained the bare citation in detail_verdict_text — a half-fix that still hands a dome defender the same rebuttal ('your second source is also JSE — why trust it?').
holes_found.recommendation: In detail_verdict_text, either (a) drop 'Kuhlke, 2010' so the citation becomes '(Bruhn, 2006)' alone, or (b) replace with '(Bruhn, 2006; Kuhlke 2010 corroborates in JSE, independently verifiable)'. Option (a) is cleanest. Bruhn's TU Darmstadt mathematical critique is self-supporting.

holes_found.description: The coupling-magnitude range 'V ≈ 20 mV to 350 mV' for drive currents of 0.1-1 A mixes geometries: the 20 mV lower bound is approximately the coplanar orientation at 0.1 A (actual: 17.6 mV), and the 350 mV upper bound is approximately the coaxial orientation at 1 A (actual: 351.9 mV). The text acknowledges the two orientations but does not make it fully transparent that the quoted range spans both. A meticulous reader could read '20-350 mV' as a single-geometry range and find the factor-of-17.5 spread implausibly large for a single setup. This is cosmetic — the physics and rounding are correct — but we criticize the dome model for sloppy numerics elsewhere.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional tightening: 'For drive currents of 0.1-1 A typical of benchtop demonstrations this gives V ≈ 20-200 mV (coplanar orientation) or 35-350 mV (coaxial orientation) — comfortably detectable...' This makes the orientation dependence explicit. Not blocking — the current phrasing is correct, just a hair loose.

holes_found.description: The stated formula 'M ≈ µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³) ≈ 4 nH (or ≈8 nH in the coaxial orientation, where the on-axis dipole field is doubled)' is slightly compressed pedagogically. As written, the formula corresponds to the coplanar orientation; the coaxial value is obtained by the factor of 2 noted in the parenthetical. Standard presentations (Jackson §5.17, Balanis §2.13) would write M_coplanar = µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³) and M_coaxial = 2·µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³). The current form is correct but requires a reader who already knows the on-axis/broadside factor. A defender with EE background could pedantically insist on explicit labeling.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: make the two geometries explicit. Low priority — the current text is correct and a technical reader will recognize the formula.

stronger_arguments.description: The c3→c4 rewrite has already absorbed the strongest previously-missing argument (quantitative near-field calculation). Remaining incremental gain would be explicit cross-reference to a second independent benchtop demonstration of near-field coupling through a cage — but this is marginal, as Maxwell-plus-Jackson-§9.1-plus-a-computation is already dispositive.
sources: Weinstein & Lafontaine (2009), 'Near-Field Wireless Power Transfer', IEEE Trans. Ind. Electron. 56(7) — or any contemporary Qi/NFC tutorial

stronger_arguments.description: Rhetorical: the operational-Faraday-shielding-across-precision-experiments argument (CUORE/LIGO/ADMX) could be sharpened by noting the sensitivity gap. LIGO's strain sensitivity is ~10⁻²³/√Hz, requiring EM isolation many orders of magnitude beyond Meyl's ~20-350 mV signal level. If scalar waves existed and penetrated copper mesh at Meyl's magnitudes, LIGO's mirror test masses would show correlated RF pickup exceeding strain tolerances by factors of 10¹⁵ or more. Quantitative 'if it existed, it would be noticed' argument.
sources: LIGO Scientific Collaboration, 'Advanced LIGO' (2015) Class. Quantum Grav. 32:074001 — strain sensitivity specs
CUORE Collaboration (2022), Nature 604:53 — EM background specs

kernel_of_truth.description: Near-field electromagnetic coupling is real and quantitative. Within the reactive zone (d < λ/2π), evanescent field components dominate, fall as 1/r³ (vs 1/r for far-field radiative components), and couple through Faraday enclosures via cable-shield capacitance, aperture flux, and finite mesh conductivity — all strictly within Maxwell's equations. Meyl's apparatus operates in exactly this regime. Qi wireless charging, NFC, and resonant inductive transfer all exploit the same physics.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Meyl correctly observed an effect; he misidentified its cause. The observation (energy detected inside a shielded enclosure at short range) is fully explained by standard near-field coupling. Meyl's interpretation (longitudinal scalar waves beyond Maxwell) is what the dome model adopts, and that interpretation is refuted both theoretically (Bruhn 2006 math) and quantitatively (our c4 near-field calculation reproduces Meyl's reported magnitudes). The dome adds no independent derivation from its own parameters — it imports Meyl's label wholesale.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender armed with c3's feedback and reading the c4 text would have to shift ground. The strongest remaining attack: 'Your calculation uses idealized point-dipole geometry (M = µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³)) valid for A << d². Meyl's actual coil dimensions (diameter ~11 cm) give d/r_coil ≈ 18 at d=1 m — borderline for the dipole approximation. At closer separations or larger coils, higher-order multipoles matter and M could exceed the leading-order estimate. Until you do the full Neumann integral or cite a matched empirical measurement, you have cited the right regime but haven't proven your numbers are conservative enough to rule out excess coupling Meyl could claim as scalar-wave evidence.' Secondary angle: 'Bruhn 2006 is a single mathematician's preprint at TU Darmstadt, not a peer-reviewed rebuttal in a physics journal.'

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph identified in c3 as a cross-WIN pattern (WIN-003, WIN-011, WIN-015) has been fully removed in all three WINs (verified: none contain 'FLAGGED' in detail_verdict_text). Clean cross-WIN fix. WIN-014 (sub-electron mass) treatment remains consistent with WIN-015 — both invoke mainstream absence-of-evidence arguments. Rhetorically uniform across the four sibling fringe-WINs.

claims_checked: µ₀·N₁N₂A₁A₂/(4πd³) ≈ 4 nH for N=20, A=100 cm², d=1 m
V = M·2πf·I at f=7 MHz: 20-350 mV across 0.1-1 A
λ, λ/2π ranges across 1-10 MHz band
Meyl cage dimensions (0.01-2 m) << λ/2π at 7 MHz (ratio ≈ 0.15)
quantitative_verification.computations: Python verification: M_coplanar = µ₀·N²·A²/(4πd³) = (4π×10⁻⁷)·400·(0.01)²/(4π·1) = 4.000 nH (exact to the stated 1 sig fig). M_coaxial = 8.000 nH. Induced EMF V = M·ω·I at f=7 MHz (ω=4.398×10⁷ rad/s): coplanar 17.6 mV (0.1 A) / 175.9 mV (1 A); coaxial 35.2 mV (0.1 A) / 351.9 mV (1 A). The text's '20-350 mV' range corresponds to coplanar lower × coaxial upper (17.6→20 rounded up, 351.9→350 rounded down). λ/2π: 47.75 m (1 MHz), 10.05 m (4.75 MHz), 6.82 m (7 MHz), 4.77 m (10 MHz) — confirms text's '~5-50 m' range within rounding. Cage ratio at 7 MHz: 1 m / 6.82 m = 0.147 — deep sub-wavelength, confirming the leakage argument.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None material. The 20 mV lower bound rounds up from 17.6 mV (2% overstatement); the 350 mV upper bound rounds down from 351.9 mV (0.5% understatement). The formula as written is the coplanar form; the coaxial value is correctly given as 2×. No arithmetic errors.

citations_verified: Jackson, Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed. 1998), §9.1 — 'Fields and Radiation of a Localized Oscillating Source' — correct reference for near-field/radiating/Fraunhofer zone definitions.
Balanis, Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design (4th ed. 2016), Ch. 2 — correct reference for near-field zone classification.
Bruhn, G.W. (2006), TU Darmstadt mathematical critique of Meyl's scalar waves — content matches our attribution.
CUORE as 'neutrinoless double-beta decay search at LNGS' — now factually correct (c3 minor hole #3 fixed).
IEEE Std 299-2006 — valid citation for EM shielding measurement methodology.
LIGO, ADMX — correct descriptions (gravitational-wave interferometry; Axion Dark Matter Experiment).
citations_failed: Kuhlke (2010) in detail_verdict_text — citation itself resolves, but JSE is a non-peer-reviewed anomalistics venue. c3 recommended dropping or annotating; c4 dropped from detail_evidence but retained in detail_verdict_text without caveat. Minor source-hygiene issue (see hole #1).
citations_unchecked: Meyl's conference proceedings (c.2000) — cited negatively, not independently re-verified.
Standard shielding table values for copper mesh >60 dB at MHz — consistent with IEEE/Balanis references but not re-measured.

tldr_check.tldr_evidence_note: Current tldr_evidence now correctly says 'mathematician at TU Darmstadt (Bruhn 2006)' and 'a back-of-envelope near-field calculation... predicts exactly the tens-to-hundreds of millivolts he reports'. Precise, quantitatively grounded, and no longer relies on 'multiple physicists' (c3 minor hole #4 fixed). Excellent TLDR.
tldr_check.tldr_verdict_note: tldr_verdict unchanged from c3 and still accurate: 'Meyl's scalar wave theory has been debunked — his mathematics is self-contradictory. The experimental observation is ordinary near-field coupling, not exotic physics.' No issues.

deeper_analysis:
c3→c4 is a clean implementation of the c3 review's three moderate and three minor recommendations. The most important change — adding a representative-geometry near-field calculation — resolves the c3 advocate-mode finding (defense_survives: 3 → 2). The defender's 'you never computed it' line is now preempted by our own computed 20-350 mV range, which matches what Meyl reports. The CUORE mislabel is fixed. The FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph is removed (and the cross-WIN fix propagated to WIN-003 and WIN-011 — verified). The TLDR is tightened to 'mathematician at TU Darmstadt' and drops 'multiple physicists'.

Two residual issues remain. First: Kuhlke (2010) in JSE remains in detail_verdict_text without the JSE caveat recommended in c3 — a half-fix. Dropping the citation entirely or adding a one-line caveat would close this. Second: the coupling-magnitude range 20-350 mV blends two orientations; while physically defensible and parenthetically acknowledged, an EE-literate reader could prefer orientations labeled separately. Both issues are minor.

Advocate mode finds the remaining defense weak (rated 2): the defender can challenge the dipole approximation (ignoring higher-order multipoles) and the single-source nature of Bruhn's critique. Neither point is strong enough to warrant a c4 text change — the dipole approximation is standard for A<<d² and Meyl's geometry (d/r_coil ≈ 18) satisfies that comfortably; Bruhn's math is transparent enough to verify independently.

Quantitative verification confirms all numerical claims within rounding. No arithmetic errors. Cross-WIN consistency improved: three fringe-claim siblings (WIN-003, 011, 015) now have uniform non-flagged treatment, matching WIN-014 template.

Recommended action: no_change. The WIN is in good shape. The two minor holes are nice-to-haves, not blockers, and can be rolled into a future housekeeping pass. Verdict 'Not Demonstrated' robustly supported.
